code_value: Hello, everyone. I'm Chen Wei, a student from Foshan. Today I'd like to tell you about a traditional art in my hometown—paper-cutting. Foshan paper-cutting has a long history of over 500 years. It is known for its beautiful patterns and bright colours. My grandmother is very good at it. She taught me when I was seven. At first, I found it difficult because the scissors were heavy. But I kept practising. Now I can make many different shapes, like flowers, animals, and Chinese characters. During the Spring Festival, we put paper-cuts on windows to bring good luck. I love this art because it shows the wisdom of our ancestors. I hope more young people will learn it.
